Plotting frame for 2D scores

Source: R/helper_score.R
dp_frame.Rd

Internal helper that constructs a plotting frame for two-dimensional score data. If frame is supplied, it is used directly. Otherwise a differentially private frame is constructed from privatized lower and upper quantiles computed from the pooled score coordinates.

Usage

dp_frame(
  X,
  eps_q = NULL,
  delta_q = NULL,
  inflate = 0.1,
  q = NULL,
  frame = NULL
)

Arguments

X

Numeric matrix with exactly two columns.

eps_q

Privacy budget for DP quantile estimation. Required only when frame = NULL.

delta_q

Privacy parameter for DP quantile estimation. Required only when frame = NULL.

inflate

Non-negative numeric value controlling frame expansion.

q

Optional symmetric quantile level in \((0, 1)\). If NULL, extreme order statistics are used.

frame

Optional user-specified frame. This can be either a numeric vector of length 2, interpreted as a common range for both axes, or a list with components xlim and ylim.

Value

A list with components xlim and ylim.
